message:
test(readme): cover module-level SQL helpers + promote to prelude

Sister to fd90.9 / fd90.10. Twelve module-level free-function SQL
helpers — paired 1:1 with SqlInspector methods — were exposed at the
crate root but absent from the prelude. The README's Quick Start
introspection surface (line 148) and the SQLAlchemy.Inspector parity
docstrings flag these as part of the user-facing API:

- list_sql_foreign_keys
- list_sql_indexes
- list_sql_schemas
- list_sql_tables
- list_sql_unique_constraints
- list_sql_views
- sql_max_identifier_length
- sql_primary_key_columns
- sql_server_version
- sql_table_comment
- sql_table_schema
- truncate_sql_table

inspect() had the same defect (fixed in fd90.10); these are the rest of
that surface.

Extends readme_quick_start_round_trip_through_sqlite to call each helper
against the parent/child schema from fd90.9 and asserts each result
agrees with its SqlInspector method counterpart. Also exercises
truncate_sql_table by inserting a row, truncating, and verifying the
table definition survives.

Closes frankenpandas-fd90.11.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -440,6 +440,7 @@
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90","title":"[EPIC] SQL backend abstraction — trait + PostgreSQL + MySQL","description":"Pandas-parity SQL IO across multiple backends. Today fp_io::read_sql / write_sql hardcode rusqlite::Connection; pandas read_sql/to_sql works with any SQLAlchemy-compatible engine (PostgreSQL, MySQL, MariaDB, MS SQL, Oracle, ...). Multi-slice umbrella; file child beads per slice.

1. pub trait SqlConnection in fp-io (DBAPI2-like surface): execute_query(sql, params) -> RowIter, execute_statement(sql, params) -> usize, with_transaction<F>(f) -> Result<T>. Minimum surface to cover rusqlite, tokio-postgres, and mysql_async concrete backends behind the same trait.

2. Cargo features: sql-sqlite (default), sql-postgresql, sql-mysql. Each feature brings its backend crate and a wrapper impl SqlConnection for that backend's Connection type. Additive; disabling sql-sqlite drops the rusqlite dep.

3. read_sql / write_sql / read_sql_table / read_sql_query signatures move from rusqlite::Connection to <C: SqlConnection>. Existing SQLite call sites keep working because rusqlite::Connection gets the wrapper impl.

4. Param binding: closes frankenpandas-tk3k by letting the public surface accept params: &[&(dyn ToSqlBindable)] routed through each backend's type-specific binder.

5. chunksize streaming: read_sql(.., chunksize=Some(n)) returns impl Iterator<Item=Result<DataFrame, ...>> to match pandas.

6. coerce_float: read_sql(.., coerce_float=true) promotes NUMERIC / DECIMAL / MONEY to Float64 at row-materialization time.

7. Live-oracle conformance: packet fixtures per backend (sqlite/postgres/mysql) verifying round-trip parity with pandas' pd.read_sql(engine) for each.

Blocker for m3e8 closure: the README now honestly says 'SQL: SQLite-only via rusqlite; PostgreSQL/MySQL planned' (commit-pending, br-m3e8). Closing that qualifier requires this epic landing — at minimum slices (1)-(3).

Decomposition strategy: (1) is the keystone — pick between (a) a minimal DBAPI2-style trait sufficient to host all three backends without leaking backend-specific types into the public API, vs (b) a typestate / erased-impl approach. (2) feature-gates each backend behind Cargo features to keep binary size bounded. (4)-(7) then unblock incrementally behind (1)+(2)+(3).","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-04-22T12:02:41.189776401Z","created_by":"cc-pandas","updated_at":"2026-04-24T00:21:21.081484312Z","close_reason":"Completed merge fuzz invariant hardening and clippy cleanup","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["backend-abstraction","epic","io","sql"]}

Include options support, umbrella re-exports, and focused fp-io tests for batching/options/zero chunksize.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T07:40:28.873856286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T07:45:27.930495933Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T07:45:27.928679878Z","close_reason":"Completed in 805b307","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","parity","sql"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.1","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T07:40:28.873856286Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.10","title":"test(readme): cover SqlInspector remaining methods (table_comment, exists, version, has_column, column, reflect_all_*, inspect)","description":"Sister to fd90.9 / fd90.290 — covers the remaining ~10 SqlInspector / inspect entry points still uncovered by integration tests:

- table_comment(table, schema)
- table_exists(table, schema)
- server_version()
- max_identifier_length()
- dialect_name()
- has_column(table, col, schema)
- column(table, col, schema)
- reflect_all_tables(schema)
- reflect_all_views(schema)
- inspect(conn) free function

These all have public-API surface and back the README's introspection claim. Extend readme_quick_start_round_trip_through_sqlite (now uses parent/child schema from fd90.9) to call each and assert results.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:33:20.779907659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T11:35:49.545692768Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T11:35:49.543700254Z","close_reason":"completed: 10 remaining SqlInspector methods covered; promoted inspect() to prelude (commit c92ad26)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.10","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:33:20.779907659Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.11","title":"test(readme): cover module-level SQL helpers (list_sql_*, sql_*) + promote to prelude","description":"Sister to fd90.9/fd90.10. The 11 module-level free-function SQL helpers — paired with SqlInspector methods — are exposed at the crate root but absent from the prelude:

- list_sql_foreign_keys
- list_sql_indexes
- list_sql_schemas
- list_sql_tables
- list_sql_unique_constraints
- list_sql_views
- sql_max_identifier_length
- sql_primary_key_columns
- sql_server_version
- sql_table_comment
- sql_table_schema

Each pairs 1:1 with a SqlInspector method (which IS in the prelude). README line 148 documents the same introspection surface. inspect() had the same defect (fixed in fd90.10) — these are the rest of that surface.

Plan:
- Add module-level coverage to readme_quick_start_round_trip_through_sqlite (call each helper against the parent/child schema and verify against the SqlInspector results from fd90.9/fd90.10)
- Promote the 11 helpers to the prelude (pairs with SqlInspector being there)","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:37:15.990474738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-27T11:37:53.395733353Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.11","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-27T11:37:15.990474738Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Reuse the table-name validation/escaping path, return SqlChunkIterator, expose the umbrella re-export, and add focused fp-io tests for batching and invalid chunksize/table names.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T07:46:41.440828863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T07:50:35.683429602Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T07:50:35.681545888Z","close_reason":"Completed in 693dc14","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","parity","sql"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.2","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T07:46:41.440828863Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Reuse table/column validation and projection query construction, return SqlChunkIterator, expose the umbrella re-export, and add focused fp-io tests for projection order, zero chunksize, and invalid projection inputs.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T07:51:48.478054749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T07:58:32.073612091Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T07:58:32.071766828Z","close_reason":"Completed in 03a4ce7","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","parity","sql"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.3","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T07:51:48.478054749Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"frankenpandas-fd90.4","title":"fp-io SQL chunks index_col helpers","description":"Child slice of frankenpandas-fd90: add chunked SQL read helpers that combine pandas-style chunksize with index_col promotion. Cover read_sql/read_sql_query/read_sql_table chunk paths by wrapping SqlChunkIterator, preserving per-chunk RangeIndex when index_col is None and promoting/removing the named column when set. Add umbrella re-exports and focused fp-io tests for query chunks, table chunks, None behavior, and missing index_col errors.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"cod-pandas","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:00:11.325655162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T08:08:08.040272870Z","closed_at":"2026-04-25T08:08:08.038384696Z","close_reason":"Completed in 90fd549","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["io","parity","sql"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"frankenpandas-fd90.4","depends_on_id":"frankenpandas-fd90","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-04-25T08:00:11.325655162Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -388,6 +388,23 @@ pub mod prelude {
         // exported at the crate root but missed prelude promotion —
         // pairs with SqlInspector being in the prelude already.
         inspect,
+        // fd90.11: module-level SQL helpers (fd90.21-32). Free-function
+        // counterparts to SqlInspector methods — paired surface, same
+        // semantics. Promote alongside SqlInspector / inspect for
+        // surface consistency. README line 148 documents the
+        // introspection surface.
+        list_sql_foreign_keys,
+        list_sql_indexes,
+        list_sql_schemas,
+        list_sql_tables,
+        list_sql_unique_constraints,
+        list_sql_views,
+        sql_max_identifier_length,
+        sql_primary_key_columns,
+        sql_server_version,
+        sql_table_comment,
+        sql_table_schema,
+        truncate_sql_table,
         read_sql_chunks,
         SqlIfExists,
         // Module-level functions (concat + join/merge family)
